engineGuide.h2: Aston Martin DB2 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Aston Martin DB2 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.6L Petrol
entries.history: Original DB2 powerplant; aluminium block with wet liners defined Aston Martin's post-war engineering.
compatibleVariants: DB2 Sports Saloon, DB2 Drophead Coupe, DB2 Vantage (125bhp), DB2 Fixed Head Coupe
commonFailures: Wet cylinder liner seal failure - coolant ingress into sump, typical after 60k miles
Sump gasket oil leaks - period cork seals degrade, requiring modern replacement
Timing chain tensioner wear - original spring units lose pressure, risking guide damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for LB6/49 Aston Martin DB2 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.6L Petrol
entries.history: High-compression evolution of LB6; enabled Vantage 125bhp output for performance buyers.
compatibleVariants: DB2 Vantage, DB2 Sports Saloon (late), DB2 Drophead Coupe (late), DB2 LM Specification
commonFailures: Cylinder head gasket failure - high compression increases risk under sustained load
Exhaust valve guide wear - accelerated on high-output applications, causes oil consumption
Oil pump wear - original gear design loses pressure after high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for LB6B/50 Aston Martin DB2 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.6L Petrol
entries.history: Prototype-specification LB6 variant; fitted to earliest LML/49 chassis pre-production units.
compatibleVariants: DB2 Prototype LML/49, DB2 Works Competition (early)
commonFailures: Wet liner cavitation - early seal design prone to erosion without coolant treatment
Carburettor synchronization - triple SU setup requires specialist tuning
Crankshaft main bearing wear - accelerated on competition-use examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for LB6/49/32 Aston Martin DB2 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Displacement increase to 3.0L for DB2/4; delivered improved torque for heavier chassis.
compatibleVariants: DB2/4 Sports Saloon, DB2/4 Drophead Coupe, DB2/4 MkII
commonFailures: Wet liner cavitation erosion - 3.0L units susceptible without proper coolant inhibitors
Cylinder head warping - larger displacement increases thermal stress on head gasket
Oil pressure drop - original pump design struggles with increased displacement demands
entries.cta: Get quotes for VB6E Aston Martin DB2 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Refined VB6E with improved breathing; standard fitment for late DB2/4 production.
compatibleVariants: DB2/4 MkII, DB2/4 MkIII, DB2/4 MkII Vantage (early), DB2/4 MkIII Vantage (early)
commonFailures: Valve guide wear - exhaust guides wear faster on high-mileage touring examples
Carburettor tuning complexity - triple SU setup demands specialist synchronization
Crankshaft main bearing fatigue - high-output applications accelerate wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for VB6J Aston Martin DB2 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: High-compression Vantage specification; triple SU carburettors delivered 165bhp for competition.
compatibleVariants: DB2/4 MkII Vantage, DB2/4 MkIII Vantage, DB2 Competition (late)
commonFailures: High-compression head gasket failure - increased cylinder pressure stresses sealing
Valve seat recession - leaded-fuel era design requires unleaded conversion for modern use
Piston ring wear - high-output tuning accelerates ring land fatigue
entries.cta: Get quotes for DBD Aston Martin DB2 engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Aston Martin DB2 engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin DB2 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin DB2's LB6 and VB6 engine families are robust but age-related failures are common. Wet cylinder liner seal failure, oil leaks, and timing chain wear dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Wet Cylinder Liner Seal Failure - Why Aston Martin DB2 Engines Suffer Coolant Ingress
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB2 Sports Saloon, Drophead Coupe, Vantage (1950-1953, LB6/49 & LB6B/50); DB2/4 variants (1953-1959, VB6E & VB6J)
problems.rootCause: Original cork/rubber liner seals degrade with age and heat cycles, allowing coolant to bypass into the sump and contaminate oil.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Seal Replacement (Engine In Situ) - Not recommended for DB2
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Attempt liner seal replacement without full strip; high risk of incomplete repair
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix only; seals often fail again within 12 months

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Partial Strip & Seal Renewal - Viable if block sound
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace liner seals, machine head if warped, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: 20,000-40,000 miles if block and liners are undamaged

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Recommended for certainty
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of fully rebuilt LB6/VB6 engine with upgraded seals, bench-tested, ready to fit
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000+ miles with proper maintenance; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a DB2 worth £80,000-£250,000, we recommend the full rebuilt engine option. Partial repairs risk hidden damage to bearings and crankshaft from coolant-contaminated oil. A specialist rebuild with upgraded seals delivers long-term reliability and preserves concours value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt LB6 engine prices for your Aston Martin DB2

problems.group: Oil Leak at Sump & Timing Cover
problems.h4: Oil Leak at Sump & Timing Cover - Why Aston Martin DB2 Engines Drip
problems.affectedModels: All Aston Martin DB2 and DB2/4 variants (1950-1959, LB6/49, LB6B/50, VB6E, VB6J, DBD)
problems.rootCause: Period cork gaskets harden and shrink with age; aluminium-to-steel joint movement breaks the seal.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - External Gasket Renewal - Viable for mild leaks
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump/timing cover, clean surfaces, fit modern composite gaskets, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: 20,000-30,000 miles if surfaces are flat and undamaged

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Modern Seals - For severe leaks or concours
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt engine with upgraded seal materials throughout, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000+ miles; eliminates multiple leak points; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: For mild external leaks on a driver-quality DB2, the minor gasket renewal is cost-effective. For concours restoration or severe leaks indicating internal wear, invest in a rebuilt engine with modern seal materials to eliminate future concerns.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DB2 sump gasket repair or engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Chain & Tensioner Wear - Why Aston Martin DB2 Engines Develop Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB2 variants with LB6/49 & LB6B/50 (1950-1953); DB2/4 with VB6 series (1953-1959)
problems.rootCause: Original spring-loaded tensioners lose pressure; chain stretches and guides wear, causing slap and potential jump.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain & Tensioner Replacement - Recommended before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain, tensioner, guides; set timing; reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: 30,000-50,000 miles if performed before catastrophic failure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If chain has jumped or damaged valves
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench-tested, ready to fit
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000+ miles; includes warranty; eliminates risk of repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If your DB2 shows early chain rattle but runs smoothly, the preventative chain replacement offers excellent value. If the chain has jumped or you hear metallic clatter on startup, assume valve or piston damage and opt for a rebuilt engine to avoid costly secondary repairs.
problems.cta: Compare timing chain service vs rebuilt engine costs for your Aston Martin DB2

problems.h4: Cylinder Head Gasket Failure - Why High-Compression DB2 Vantage Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB2 Vantage, DB2/4 MkII Vantage, DB2/4 MkIII Vantage (LB6B/50, DBD engines)
problems.rootCause: High compression ratios (8.0:1 to 9.0:1) increase cylinder pressure; age-hardened gaskets fail under thermal cycling.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Head Removal & Gasket Renewal - Viable if block/head are sound
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine if warped, fit modern multi-layer gasket, reassemble with torque specs
repairOptions.longevity: 30,000-50,000 miles if no underlying damage to head or block

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt High-Compression Engine - For concours or repeated failures
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt Vantage-spec engine with upgraded head studs, modern gaskets, bench-tested

problems.recommendation: On a Vantage-spec DB2 worth £150,000+, invest in the rebuilt high-compression engine to preserve performance and value. For driver-quality cars with a single gasket failure and no overheating history, the intermediate head service may suffice if performed by a DB2 specialist.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DB2 Vantage engine rebuild with high-compression specification

problems.h4: Carburettor Tuning & Ignition Issues - Why Aston Martin DB2 Engines Run Poorly
problems.affectedModels: All Aston Martin DB2 and DB2/4 variants with triple SU carburettors (1950-1959)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; tuning issues are maintenance-related, not wear-related
problems.rootCause: Triple SU carburettors require precise synchronization; age-related wear in linkages and jets causes imbalance.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Carburettor Rebuild & Synchronization - Recommended for poor running
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, strip, clean, rebuild SU carbs with new jets/seals; synchronize on flow bench; refit and tune
repairOptions.longevity: Indefinite with annual tuning; resolves poor running, hesitation, and fuel economy issues

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Carb Service - If poor running indicates internal wear
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt engine with carburettors serviced or replaced, bench-tested, ready to fit
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000+ miles; eliminates uncertainty about internal condition; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: Start with the carburettor rebuild and synchronization-most poor-running DB2s are fixed this way. Only consider engine replacement if compression tests reveal internal wear or if the car has a history of overheating that may have caused hidden damage.
problems.cta: Compare SU carburettor service vs full engine rebuild quotes for your Aston Martin DB2

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin DB2
finalCta.paragraph: The Aston Martin DB2's classic LB6 and VB6 engines are robust but age-related failures are inevitable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Aston Martin DB2 specialists who understand period-correct rebuilds. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and preserve your DB2's value without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin DB2 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin DB2 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Aston Martin DB2 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Aston Martin DB2 is listed below, with compatible LB6/VB6 engine codes, production years and common failure notes for accurate replacement identification.

groups.title: Aston Martin DB2 Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Aston Martin DB2 2.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.6-litre LB6 straight-six powered all original DB2 models from 1950-1953, delivering 105bhp in standard form or 125bhp in Vantage specification. This aluminium-block engine with wet cylinder liners defined Aston Martin's post-war engineering and remains the most commonly replaced unit in the DB2 range.
commonFailurePoints: Wet liner seal failure causing coolant ingress; sump gasket oil leaks; timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileage
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB2 2.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin DB2 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre VB6 series engines were introduced for the DB2/4 in 1953, providing increased torque for the heavier chassis and enabling the 140bhp standard and 165bhp Vantage outputs. These refined LB6 derivatives feature improved breathing and remain highly sought after for concours and performance restorations.
commonFailurePoints: Wet liner cavitation erosion without proper coolant treatment; valve guide wear on high-mileage examples; carburettor synchronization complexity
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB2 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin DB2 Petrol Engines
items.description: All Aston Martin DB2 and DB2/4 variants were powered exclusively by naturally aspirated petrol straight-six engines, with displacements of 2.6L (LB6 series) and 3.0L (VB6 series) across the 1950-1959 production run.
items.descriptor: All Aston Martin DB2 and DB2/4 variants were powered exclusively by naturally aspirated petrol straight-six engines, with displacements of 2.6L (LB6 series) and 3.0L (VB6 series) across the 1950-1959 production run.
items.familiesLabel: Common Aston Martin DB2 Petrol Engine Families
families: LB6/49 - original 2.6L DB2 engine (105bhp), most common replacement unit
LB6B/50 - high-compression 2.6L Vantage engine (125bhp), performance specialist rebuilds
VB6J - refined 3.0L DB2/4 engine (140bhp), improved torque for heavier chassis
DBD - triple-carb 3.0L Vantage engine (165bhp), competition-specification rebuilds
foundIn: DB2/4 Sports Saloon/Drophead/MkII/MkIII (1953-1959) - VB6E, VB6J, DBD

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin DB2 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Aston Martin DB2 and DB2/4 production timeline (1950-1959), organised so owners can quickly identify their LB6 or VB6 engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original DB2 debuted with the LB6/49 2.6L straight-six, establishing the wet-liner aluminium block architecture that would define the model.
keyChanges: DB2 Sports Saloon and Drophead Coupe enter production at Feltham
LB6/49 engine introduced: 2580cc, 105bhp, triple SU carburettors
Wet cylinder liner design adopted for serviceability and weight savings
mainEngines: LB6/49 - 2.6L petrol (DB2 Sports Saloon / Drophead Coupe)
knownFor: Groundbreaking aluminium construction for a production GT
Early wet liner seal issues on high-mileage examples
Strong collector demand for original, matching-numbers engines
Specialist rebuild knowledge essential for authentic restoration
years.cta: Get quotes for 1950-1951 Aston Martin DB2 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB2 LB6 engine rebuild cost • DB2 wet liner seal failure • DB2 matching numbers engine

years.description: High-compression LB6B/50 engines enabled the 125bhp Vantage specification, creating the DB2's performance flagship.
keyChanges: LB6B/50 high-compression engine introduced for Vantage models
Cylinder head revised for improved breathing and 125bhp output
Final DB2 production year before DB2/4 transition
knownFor: Vantage specification commands premium in collector market
High-compression engines more prone to head gasket failure
Matching-numbers Vantage engines exceptionally valuable
Specialist knowledge required for authentic high-compression rebuilds
years.cta: Get quotes for 1952-1953 Aston Martin DB2 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB2 Vantage engine rebuild • LB6B head gasket failure • DB2 high-compression engine

years.description: The DB2/4 introduced the enlarged 3.0L VB6E engine, providing increased torque for the heavier, more practical grand tourer chassis.
keyChanges: DB2/4 model launched with longer wheelbase and 2+2 seating
VB6E 3.0L engine introduced: 2922cc, 140bhp, refined LB6 architecture
Wet liner design retained but with improved sealing specifications
mainEngines: VB6E - 3.0L petrol (DB2/4 Sports Saloon / early Drophead)
knownFor: 3.0L displacement better suited to DB2/4's increased weight
Wet liner cavitation risk without proper coolant inhibitor use
Transition year creates identification complexity for restorers
Specialist rebuilders familiar with both LB6 and VB6 architectures preferred
years.cta: Get quotes for 1954-1955 Aston Martin DB2/4 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB2/4 3.0L engine rebuild • VB6E wet liner failure • DB2/4 engine identification

years.description: The DB2/4 MkII standardised the refined VB6J engine, improving reliability and drivability across the range.
keyChanges: DB2/4 MkII introduced with revised styling and chassis updates
VB6J engine becomes standard: improved breathing, 140bhp output
DB2/4 MkII Vantage introduced with DBD 165bhp high-compression engine
knownFor: VB6J considered the most balanced DB2/4 engine for touring use
DBD Vantage engines highly sought after for competition restorations
Triple SU carburettor synchronization critical for optimal performance
Concours restorers prioritize period-correct engine codes and specifications
years.cta: Get quotes for 1956-1957 Aston Martin DB2/4 MkII replacement engines
years.ticker: DB2/4 MkII engine rebuild • DBD Vantage engine specialist • VB6J carburettor tuning

years.preview: DB2/4 MkIII Final Production: VB6J/DBD Carry-Over
years.description: The final DB2/4 MkIII retained the proven VB6J and DBD engines, closing production of the LB6/VB6 family before the DB4's introduction.
keyChanges: DB2/4 MkIII introduced with minor styling and trim updates
Engine lineup unchanged: VB6J standard, DBD for Vantage
Final year of DB2/4 production before DB4 launch in 1958
knownFor: Final iteration of the LB6/VB6 engine family; highly collectible
Low production numbers increase rarity of original engines
Matching-numbers MkIII Vantage engines command exceptional premiums
Specialist rebuilders with DB2/4 MkIII experience preferred for authenticity
years.cta: Get quotes for 1958-1959 Aston Martin DB2/4 MkIII replacement engines
years.ticker: DB2/4 MkIII engine rebuild • DBD Vantage matching numbers • DB2/4 final production engine

faq.h2: Aston Martin DB2 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to rebuild an Aston Martin DB2 engine?
items.answer: A full specialist rebuild of an Aston Martin DB2 LB6 or VB6 engine typically costs £8,000-£25,000 depending on specification, with Vantage and concours-grade rebuilds at the upper end.
keyPoints: LB6/49 standard rebuilds start around £8,000 for driver-quality specification
LB6B/50 Vantage rebuilds range £12,000-£22,000 due to high-compression components
VB6J and DBD 3.0L rebuilds command £15,000-£25,000 for period-correct work
Matching-numbers or concours-spec rebuilds may exceed £25,000
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Aston Martin DB2 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most common engine failure on the Aston Martin DB2?
items.answer: Wet cylinder liner seal failure is the most frequently reported catastrophic issue, allowing coolant into the oil sump and risking bearing damage if not addressed promptly.
keyPoints: Original cork/rubber liner seals degrade with age and heat cycles
Coolant contamination of oil accelerates bearing and crankshaft wear
Upgraded modern seal materials are recommended during any rebuild
Early detection via oil inspection can prevent catastrophic failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DB2 wet liner seal repair or engine replacement

items.question: Can I use a non-original engine code in my Aston Martin DB2?
items.answer: While LB6 and VB6 engines share architecture, using a non-original code affects matching-numbers status and collector value; period-correct rebuilds are strongly recommended.
keyPoints: LB6/49, LB6B/50, VB6E, VB6J and DBD have distinct specifications
Concours and investment-grade restorations require matching engine codes
Driver-quality cars may accept compatible codes with disclosure
Always consult a DB2 specialist before substituting engine families
items.cta: Enquire about period-correct Aston Martin DB2 engine rebuild options

items.question: How long does an Aston Martin DB2 engine rebuild take?
items.answer: A full specialist rebuild of an LB6 or VB6 engine typically takes 8-16 weeks depending on parts availability, specification complexity and workshop workload.
keyPoints: Driver-quality rebuilds may complete in 8-10 weeks
Concours or Vantage-spec rebuilds often require 12-16 weeks
Rare parts sourcing (e.g., Vantage pistons) can extend timelines
Reputable specialists provide regular progress updates
items.cta: Check Aston Martin DB2 engine rebuild availability and lead times

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Aston Martin DB2 engine?
items.answer: Reputable UK specialists typically offer a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt LB6/VB6 engines, covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary by specialist; always confirm in writing
Coverage typically excludes damage from improper installation or maintenance
Some specialists offer extended warranty options for additional cost
EngineMarket network suppliers all provide minimum 12-month coverage
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Aston Martin DB2 rebuilt engine quotes

items.question: Is it worth repairing or replacing a failed Aston Martin DB2 engine?
items.answer: For DB2s valued £80,000+, a full rebuilt engine is usually the most cost-effective long-term solution versus repeated partial repairs.
keyPoints: Partial repairs risk hidden damage from coolant-contaminated oil
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failure points
A warranty on a rebuilt unit provides peace of mind versus spot repairs
Preserve collector value with period-correct, matching-numbers rebuilds
items.cta: Get a tailored Aston Martin DB2 repair vs replace recommendation

items.question: Where is the engine number located on an Aston Martin DB2?
items.answer: The LB6/VB6 engine number is stamped on a plate attached to the left-hand side of the cylinder block, near the distributor drive.
keyPoints: Format typically: LB6/49/XXXX or VB6J/XXXX (XXXX = serial)
Photograph the plate before engine removal for rebuild reference
Cross-check with chassis number for matching-numbers verification
Specialists can assist with decoding if the plate is damaged
items.cta: Verify your Aston Martin DB2 engine code before requesting quotes

items.question: Can an Aston Martin DB2 engine run on unleaded fuel?
items.answer: Original LB6/VB6 engines were designed for leaded fuel; unleaded conversion via hardened valve seats is recommended for modern use.
keyPoints: Soft exhaust valve seats wear rapidly on unleaded without conversion
Unleaded conversion involves machining and inserting hardened seats
Many rebuilt engines now include this upgrade as standard
Lead replacement additives are a temporary solution only
items.cta: Enquire about unleaded conversion during your Aston Martin DB2 engine rebuild

items.question: What oil specification should I use in a rebuilt Aston Martin DB2 engine?
items.answer: Use a high-quality 20W-50 mineral or semi-synthetic oil formulated for classic engines, with ZDDP additives to protect flat-tappet camshafts.
keyPoints: Avoid modern low-viscosity oils; they lack film strength for classic bearings
ZDDP (zinc) content is critical for camshaft and lifter protection
Change oil and filter after the first 500 miles of running-in
Follow specialist recommendations for break-in procedure
items.cta: Get Aston Martin DB2 engine rebuild quotes with running-in guidance

items.question: How do I find a trustworthy Aston Martin DB2 engine specialist?
items.answer: Look for specialists with proven DB2/LB6/VB6 experience, transparent pricing, warranty coverage, and positive owner testimonials.
keyPoints: Ask for examples of previous DB2 rebuilds and references
Confirm they use upgraded components for known failure points
Verify warranty terms and post-rebuild support policies
EngineMarket vets all network specialists for expertise and reliability
items.cta: Compare trusted Aston Martin DB2 engine specialist quotes today
